artifacts.claimText: For 2025 undergraduate graduation design/thesis work, NUAA says AI tools may only assist research and writing, may not replace independent thinking, and AI-generated content should be separated from personal academic contribution and clearly marked.

artifacts.claimText: For 2025 undergraduate graduation design/thesis work, NUAA prohibits fully relying on AI for thesis/design writing or research and prohibits using unverified AI-generated content to fabricate data, alter results, or evade plagiarism checks.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于在2025届本科毕业设计（论文）中规范AI工具使用的通知
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 禁止完全依赖AI工具进行论文（设计）写作或研究，不得使用AI工具进行研究方案设计、创新性方法设计、算法框架搭建、论文结构设计、数据分析、创新性总结等。...禁止直接使用未经核实的AI工具生成内容，利用AI工具编造、伪造研究数据或篡改实验结果、原始图片等。...禁止通过AI工具对论文进行“同义改写”“结构重组”等操作以规避查重检测。

artifacts.title: Nanjing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Official NUAA evidence found for 2025 undergraduate graduation design/thesis AI tool use. Claims are limited to the Academic Affairs Office notice: AI tools are auxiliary, prohibited uses include core content generation, fabrication and plagiarism-check evasion, confidential/private data must not be uploaded to AI platforms, and AIGC spot-check thresholds are described for thesis defense eligibility.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No broader university-wide, graduate, staff, procurement, or research AI policy was confirmed in this run.
aao.nuaa.edu.cn/robots.txt returned 404 during the check; the official page itself was publicly accessible by HTTP and Firecrawl.
Firecrawl search and scrape were used successfully; no Playwright escalation was needed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
